seoTitle: Pitt Town vs Pleasant Hills — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Pitt Town and Pleasant Hills. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Pitt Town vs Pleasant Hills: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pitt Town and Pleasant Hills.
heading: Pitt Town vs Pleasant Hills — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pitt Town, New South Wales and Pleasant Hills, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pleasant Hills has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Pitt Town (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pitt Town or Pleasant Hills safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pitt Town has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Pleasant Hills scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pitt Town scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Pleasant Hills scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pitt Town or Pleasant Hills?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pleasant Hills has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
